lede: A look at April 1 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Rachel Maddow and Sophie Germain, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Google
events.desc: Google launches its Email service Gmail.

events.title: Same-sex marriage in the Netherlands
events.desc: Same-sex marriage becomes legal in the Netherlands, the first contemporary country to allow it.

events.title: Lockheed EP-3
events.desc: An EP-3E United States Navy surveillance aircraft collides with a Chinese People's Liberation Army Shenyang J-8 fighter jet. The Chinese pilot ejected but is subsequently lost. The Navy crew makes an emergency landing in Hainan, China and is detained.

events.title: Steve Jobs
events.desc: Steve Jobs and Steve Wozniak found Apple Computer, Inc.

events.title: Richard Nixon
events.desc: President Richard Nixon signs the Public Health Cigarette Smoking Act into law.

events.title: Hawker Siddeley Harrier
events.desc: The Hawker Siddeley Harrier, the first operational fighter aircraft with Vertical/Short Takeoff and Landing capabilities, enters service with the Royal Air Force.

events.title: Fântâna Albă massacre
events.desc: Fântâna Albă massacre: Between two hundred and two thousand Romanian civilians are killed by Soviet Border Troops.

events.title: Northern Ireland
events.desc: In newly formed Northern Ireland, six Catholics are murdered in the Arnon Street killings, one week after six others were killed in the McMahon killings.

events.title: Royal Air Force
events.desc: The Royal Air Force is created by the merger of the Royal Flying Corps and the Royal Naval Air Service.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: Union troops led by Philip Sheridan decisively defeat Confederate troops led by George Pickett, cutting the Army of Northern Virginia's last supply line during the Siege of Petersburg.

births.name: Otto von Bismarck
births.line: German lawyer and politician, 1st Chancellor of the German Empire

births.name: Samuel Alito
births.line: American lawyer and jurist, Associate Justice of the Supreme Court of the United States

births.name: Gaston Eyskens
births.line: Belgian economist and politician, 47th Prime Minister of Belgium

deaths.text: Kathleen Lonsdale, Irish crystallographer and prison reformer
Charles I of Austria, emperor of Austria
Sigismund I the Old, king of Poland

observances.text: Christian feast day: Frederick Denison Maurice (Church of England)
Christian feast day: Walric, abbot of Leuconay
